title: podman-run option to specify the network namespace the default bridge should be created in
body:
### Feature request description

An option like `podman run --network=bridge:bridge_network_namespace=/run/netns/vpnns` would be useful to direct all container traffic via a vpn connection. Any `--publish` ports should be forwarded from the interfaces/IPs in vpnns, not the root network namespace.



### Suggest potential solution

If netavark sees this option, maybe it could change its network namespace first before it works on the setup/teardown of bridge, nftables rules etc. 

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Right now, to achieve this same goal, I use `NetworkNamespacePath=/run/netns/vpnns` with `PrivateMounts=no` in my systemd units that use `podman run`. This requires the user to prefix all future podman interactions with the container (inspect/exec/logs/kill etc) with `nsenter --net=/run/netns/vpnns` . When there are multiple such vpn namespaces, this gets messy and it's easy to make mistakes.

### Additional context

There are other solutions that allow for directing all container traffic via a vpnns (eg: use an unmanaged bridge in the root namespace that directs its traffic via the vpnns), but none of them work as [effortlessly](https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/36174) as netavark for publishing ports from the external IPs of the vpnns.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
